title: Advocacy * Sexual Assault Issues - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Legal & Public Safety > Advocacy * Sexual Assault Issues 7 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - Children and Youth With Disabilities/Health Conditions Disabilities Issues Domestic Violence Issues Food Stamps/SNAP Recipients Former Foster Children Foster Children Hunger/Food Issues Sexual Assault Issues Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF CALL TO SAFETY SEXUAL ASSAULT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides emotional support, safety planning, crisis intervention, information and referrals, and education through the support line. In-person hospital response for sexual assault is also available through the Unified Response Group. PO Box 42610 Portland, OR 97242 Eligibility: Survivors of sexual assault or violence Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 235-5333 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HENDERSON HOUSE HENDERSON HOUSE ADVOCACY CENTER CRISIS INTERVENTION SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides domestic violence and sexual assault client advocacy services. Services include a crisis hotline, coordinated wraparound service, referrals, assistance with filling out protective orders, youth outreach support groups, weekly support groups for domestic violence and sexual violence survivors (including adult survivors of child sexual abuse), and an art therapy group. 610 SE 1st Street McMinnville, OR 97128 Eligibility: Survivors of interpersonal and/or sexual violence (including stalking), human trafficking, and their children who have experienced or witnessed abuse Hours: Crisis line: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Support groups: Tuesday/ Thursday 6pm-7:30pm All other services: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (503) 472-1503 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MAYDAY INC Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a confidential hotline for survivors of domestic violence, sexual assault, and elder abuse. Services include emergency shelter, assistance with filing restraining orders, transportation to healthcare facilities and court, advocacy, and legal support. Additional services include information and referrals, community education, training programs, and court advocacy. 1705 Main Street Baker City, OR 97814 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Crisis line hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Office hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (541) 523-4134 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SABLE HOUSE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers case management, safety planning, information and referral, advocacy, support groups, community outreach, and education related to domestic and sexual violence. Support groups are available both to the public and to residents of the confidential shelter facility. Also provides rental and housing assistance services. 314 SE Oak Street Dallas, OR 97338 Eligibility: Survivors of domestic or sexual violence and their children Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 623-6703 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SAFE OF COLUMBIA COUNTY SUPPORT GROUPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers support groups and community education on sexual harassment, sexual assault, and domestic violence and adult domestic violence prevention workshops. Provides medical, legal, and financial advocacy for survivors such as navigating the Department of Human Services Child Welfare and the Self Sufficiency office. Assists with applying for Temporary Assistance for Domestic Violence Survivors (TA-DVS). Rural outreach: Supports survivors with safety planning, support groups, and meets for weekly consultation. 2194 Columbia Boulevard Saint Helens, OR 97051 Eligibility: Survivors of domestic and/or sexual violence, stalking, and sex trafficking Hours: Varies Email (503) 397-6161 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SIUSLAW OUTREACH SERVICES SIUSLAW AREA RESOURCE CENTER DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SUPPORT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a resource, referral, and crisis line that primarily focuses on survivors of domestic/sexual violence, and human trafficking and stalking. In addition to providing a crisis line, offers support in the form of a domestic violence safe house, protection orders, advocacy, and support groups. 1536 W 12th Street Florence, OR 97439 Eligibility: Primarily focuses on survivors of domestic/sexual violence; other emergency calls are accepted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 997-4444 24- Get Directions Visit Website More Details THE HARBOR Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a day center, domestic and sexual violence advocacy, culturally specific LGBTQ2+ and Latinx services, relocation assistance, transportation to a safe location, accompaniment to medical or forensic exams, assistance applying for Crime Victims Compensation, and information and referral to community agencies. Provides educational programs. These programs focus on raising awareness about the dynamics of domestic and sexual violence, understanding trauma, and promoting prevention strategies within the community. Currently accepting volunteers. 801 Commercial Street Astoria, OR 97103 Eligibility: Age 14 and older Hours: Advocacy Office: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday/Friday 9am-4pm, Wednesday 9am-noon, closed 1st Wednesday of every month. Online Chat and Text: Monday-Friday 10am-5pm Email (503) 325-5735 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CALL TO SAFETY SEXUAL ASSAULT SERVICES HENDERSON HOUSE CRISIS INTERVENTION SERVICES MAYDAY INC MAYDAY INC SABLE HOUSE SABLE HOUSE SAFE OF COLUMBIA COUNTY SUPPORT GROUPS SIUSLAW OUTREACH SERVICES DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SUPPORT THE HARBOR THE HARBOR
